    <h1 {...{
      "id": "javascript-modules"
    }}>{`JavaScript Modules`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to organize your plugin code across multiple JavaScript files using modules`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP plugins support modular JavaScript development, allowing you to organize your code across multiple files for better maintainability and structure. This is particularly valuable for complex plugins where separating functionality into distinct modules improves code organization and reusability.`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP uses the CommonJS module system with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` statements for importing modules and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`module.exports`}</inlineCode>{` for exporting functionality.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implementation"
    }}>{`Implementation`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "exporting-modules"
    }}>{`Exporting Modules`}</h3>
    <p>{`To make functions, objects, or variables available to other files, use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`module.exports`}</inlineCode>{` in your module file:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "utilsjs"
    }}>{`utils.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Export individual functions
function multiply(a, b) {
    return a * b;
}

function getAnswer() {
    return 42;
}

// Export an object containing all functions
module.exports = {
    multiply,
    getAnswer
};
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "importing-modules"
    }}>{`Importing Modules`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` statement to import modules into your main file or other modules:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Import the entire module object
const utils = require("./utils.js");
const result = utils.multiply(3, 2); // returns 6

// Or use destructuring to import specific functions
const { multiply, getAnswer } = require("./utils.js");
const answer = getAnswer(); // returns 42
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "directory-structure"
    }}>{`Directory Structure`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can organize modules in subdirectories using relative paths:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`my-plugin/
├── index.js
├── lib/
│   ├── calculations.js
│   └── helpers.js
└── components/
    └── ui-elements.js
`}</code></pre>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Import from subdirectories
const { calculate } = require("./lib/calculations.js");
const { createButton } = require("./components/ui-elements.js");
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Path Requirements`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` function in UXP uses relative paths and doesn't search global paths. Always specify the complete relative path from the current file to the target module, including the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`.js`}</inlineCode>{` extension.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h2>
    <p>{`Here's a practical example of a plugin that uses multiple modules to organize different aspects of functionality:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="3" languages="JavaScript, JavaScript, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-2"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
const { processVideo } = require("./lib/video-processor.js");
const { showNotification } = require("./lib/ui-helpers.js");

entrypoints.setup({
    commands: {
        processCommand: async () => {
            try {
                await processVideo();
                showNotification("Video processing completed!");
            } catch (error) {
                showNotification("Error: " + error.message);
            }
        }
    }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "libvideo-processorjs"
    }}>{`lib/video-processor.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`async function processVideo() {
    // Video processing logic here
    console.log("Processing video...");
    // Simulate async operation
    return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
}

function getVideoInfo() {
    // Return video information
    return { duration: 120, format: "mp4" };
}

module.exports = {
    processVideo,
    getVideoInfo
};
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "libui-helpersjs"
    }}>{`lib/ui-helpers.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"

    console.log(\`Notification: \${message}\`);
    // Additional UI notification logic
}

function createProgressBar() {
    // Progress bar creation logic
    return document.createElement("progress");
}

module.exports = {
    showNotification,
    createProgressBar
};
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p>{`JavaScript modules in UXP plugins enable better code organization and maintainability by allowing you to split functionality across multiple files.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Key Concepts:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Module exports`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`module.exports`}</inlineCode>{` to expose functions, objects, or variables from a module file.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Module imports`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`require()`}</inlineCode>{` with relative paths to import modules into other files.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Directory structure`}</strong>{`: Organize modules in subdirectories using relative paths for better project structure.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Path requirements`}</strong>{`: Always specify complete relative paths including the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.js`}</inlineCode>{` extension—UXP doesn't search global paths.`}</li>
